evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Enforce data quality through testing, validation, and monitoring
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Responsibilities emphasise ownership of data quality, testing, and documentation with no velocity/shipping-speed language present.
evidence.domain_risk: Partner with Data Engineer on modeling standards; Work with business stakeholders to create data products
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Errors would affect internal business decisions and self-service analytics but there is no financial reporting, compliance, or regulatory language.
evidence.collaboration_width: Data Engineer; business stakeholders (product, growth, finance, operations)
evidence.data_team_maturity: Own Rebtel's semantic layer with LookML and Looker platform administration
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Named tools (dbt, LookML, Looker, Snowflake) appear in responsibilities with an existing semantic layer to own and extend, indicating a mid-stage, growing data function.
evidence.jd_authorship: Design and maintain dbt transformations powering self-service analytics; Optimize query performance on Snowflake
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Responsibilities name specific tools and precise technical actions (dbt, LookML, Snowflake query optimization), letting the reader understand a typical day's work.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Own Rebtel's semantic layer with LookML and Looker platform administration; Drive data documentation and literacy
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The primary audience is the internal data platform and its consumers (self-service analytics, documentation, literacy) rather than a single commercial or finance function.
evidence.autonomy_level: Own Rebtel's semantic layer with LookML and Looker platform administration
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: The verb "own" applied to the semantic layer signals the candidate sets direction for a technical domain rather than merely executing scoped tasks.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: No mention of AI tools for the candidate's own work or AI systems consuming the candidate's data output.
evidence.testing_framing: Enforce data quality through testing, validation, and monitoring
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Testing and data quality are framed as an active, owned responsibility ("enforce") rather than a listed skill.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Enforce data quality through testing, validation, and monitoring
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Data quality and trustworthiness are emphasised ("makes data trustworthy") but there is no compliance, regulatory, or stakeholder-trust risk register, so this is moderate rather than high.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Design and maintain dbt transformations powering self-service analytics
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: The role both designs new transformations and maintains/owns an existing semantic layer, giving a mixed greenfield and fix/scale signal.
evidence.loss_aversion: "you are the person who makes data trustworthy and usable at scale" — quality/trust framing is present but secondary to delivery, with no compliance or regulatory register.
